← All Digests Indian Parliament news
Mar 25
Daily Intelligence Brief

Indian Parliament Session

Wednesday, March 25, 2026
Activity 4 / 5

Key Bills & Legislation

Appropriation Bill 2026 Mar 18 — PASSED Both Houses passed, authorising ₹53.5 lakh crore withdrawal from the Consolidated Fund for FY 2025-26. FM Sitharaman cited capex surge to ₹12.20 lakh crore (4.2× from 2017-18). Classified as a Money Bill under Article 114.
Finance Bill 2026 Mar 23 — TABLED FM Sitharaman introduced the Finance Bill in Lok Sabha. Key changes: tax-free income up to ₹12 lakh under new regime, MAT rate cut from 15% to 14%, TDS/TCS rationalisations, customs tariff simplification, and advance ruling validity extended from 3 to 5 years.
Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill 2026 Mar 23 — SENT TO JPC Amends LLP Act 2008 & Companies Act 2013 to ease rules for startups and small businesses. Lok Sabha moved it to a Joint Parliamentary Committee. Congress MP Manish Tewari opposed, citing unconstitutional delegation to subordinate legislation.
Transgender Persons Amendment Bill 2026 Mar 13 — UNDER DEBATE Revises transgender definition, requires District Magistrate-mandated medical board certification, and explicitly excludes persons based on sexual orientation. Opposition MPs and activists demand withdrawal, calling it an erasure of self-identification rights from the 2019 Act.

Debates & Issues

Speech
PM Modi on West Asia Crisis — Rajya Sabha
On March 24, PM Modi told the Rajya Sabha that the West Asia war has "created a serious energy crisis." Over 3,75,000 Indians evacuated; India expanded energy imports from 27 to 41 countries; strategic petroleum reserves being expanded to 65 lakh MT. CPI(M)'s John Brittas criticised the format, noting the opposition was not permitted to seek clarifications.
Party
Opposition Moves to Remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar
INDIA bloc filed removal notices in both Houses — 130 Lok Sabha MPs and 63 Rajya Sabha MPs — seeking removal of Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar over alleged constitutional and procedural violations.
Policy
LPG Shortage & Price Hike Adjournment Motion
Congress MP Vijay Vasanth moved an adjournment motion in Lok Sabha over LPG cylinder shortages and allegations of underweight cylinders at higher prices. Oil companies have cut commercial LPG supply due to the Strait of Hormuz disruption, prioritising household users.
Outcome
All-Party Meeting Called on Iran War
The Centre convened an all-party meeting for 5 PM, March 25, in the Parliament complex amid mounting opposition criticism of the government's handling of the West Asia conflict's economic fallout for India.

GitHub Picks

4 picks
datameet/india-election-data
~800★ Easy
Curated Lok Sabha & state assembly election datasets — candidates, affidavits, vote counts.
Essential ground-truth data for cross-referencing MP backgrounds with parliamentary votes.
CivicDataLab/opub
~120★ Medium
Open-source data publishing platform for structured government, budget, and legislation datasets.
Powers Open Budgets India; useful for self-hosting parliamentary data in machine-readable form.
justicehub-in
Org Medium
Open datasets on Indian courts, legislative texts, and legal access — built by civic technologists.
Relevant now as the Trans Amendment Bill's constitutionality heads toward legal scrutiny.
github.com/topics/parliament (Python)
Topic Easy
Curated Python projects: parliamentary debate scrapers, vote-tracking bots, bill-status APIs.
Quick starting point to build a budget-session tracker or Finance Bill alert system.

Comprehensive coverage of all three hot-button issues in both Houses on March 24, including the all-party meeting notice. Read →
⚖ The Leaflet Legal Analysis
Architecture of Erasure: How the Trans Amendment Bill 2026 erases those it claims to protect
Critical legal analysis arguing that mandatory medical board certification undoes the self-identification rights granted by the Transgender Persons Act 2019. Read →
📖 Outlook India Opinion
Trans Amendment Bill 2026: How State Surveillance Can Deny Rights to The 'Genuinely Oppressed'
Explores how the new District Magistrate certification regime could be weaponised as a gatekeeping mechanism. Read →

Tip of the Day

Track any bill's full parliamentary journey using PRS India's BillTrack.

Visit prsindia.org/billtrack for live status of every bill — from introduction through committee referral, Lok Sabha passage, Rajya Sabha passage, and Presidential assent. Each bill page includes the full text, committee reports, a plain-language summary, and key issues raised.

Both the Finance Bill 2026 and Transgender Persons Amendment Bill 2026 are currently listed there with their current stage. Subscribe to daily session alerts at PRS Session Track to get a digest of each day's proceedings in your inbox.

Budget Session 2026 enters its final stretch — Finance Bill under debate, Trans Amendment Bill sparks rights controversy, and PM Modi's West Asia energy briefing dominates the Upper House.
